title: REST integration walk-through
summary: Explain endpoints, auth, payloads, validation, logging, and retries in a simple interview-ready flow.
questions: How would you explain a REST integration end to end?
What do you validate before calling an API?
codeSnippet: POST /api/example\nHeaders: Authorization, Content-Type\nValidate payload -> send -> handle response -> log outcome

title: Notification automation with saved searches
summary: Describe how saved searches, email templates, and scheduled actions combine for recurring communications.
questions: How do you send a weekly open-ticket summary?
How do you avoid sending the wrong audience?
codeSnippet: savedSearch -> emailTemplate -> scheduled action -> recipient validation

title: Approval routing logic
summary: Talk through branching approvals, approver determination, fallback handling, and communication design.
questions: How do you route approvals by request type?
What happens when an approver is unavailable?
codeSnippet: if request.type == 'High Risk': approvers = CAB\nelse: approvers = manager

title: Validation logic design
summary: Describe how to place and scope validations so they protect data without breaking good user flow.
questions: Where do you place validation logic?
How do you keep validation maintainable?
codeSnippet: if missing required value for current stage: block save and show clear message

title: Workflow exception handling
summary: Explain how you design for failed steps, missing data, rejected approvals, and manual recovery paths.
questions: How do you handle workflow exceptions?
What should be logged for automation troubleshooting?
codeSnippet: on failure -> capture context -> alert owner -> route to manual recovery path

title: Data mapping strategy
summary: Discuss identifiers, transformation, defaults, null handling, and reconciliation-safe updates.
questions: What matters most in data mapping?
How do you avoid duplicate creation?
codeSnippet: target.status = mapStatus(source.status)\nif missing identifier -> hold for review

title: Retry and backoff patterns
summary: Describe safe retries, backoff, duplicate prevention, and alerting.
questions: When should you retry?
How do you avoid duplicate side effects?
codeSnippet: retry 3 times with backoff; stop on permanent failure; log correlation id

title: Endpoint targeting strategy
summary: Explain group design, pilot selection, exclusions, and change control for endpoint actions.
questions: How do you design targeting groups?
Why are exclusions important?

title: Patch ring rollout design
summary: Discuss pilot devices, success gates, exceptions, and phased deployment decision making.
questions: How do you decide when to move from pilot to broad rollout?

codeSnippet: pilot -> wave1 -> wave2 with gates on success rate and critical failures

title: Software deployment failure triage
summary: Walk through logs, targeting, packaging, agent state, and remediation actions.
questions: How do you troubleshoot a failed software rollout?

codeSnippet: check target, package, agent, log, exit code, connectivity

title: Enrollment troubleshooting
summary: Explain common enrollment blockers, ownership mismatches, and baseline policy issues.
questions: What are common enrollment failures?

codeSnippet: verify ownership, profile, connectivity, enrollment status, baseline policy

title: Compliance remediation design
summary: Describe user notifications, grace periods, restrictions, and recovery flows.
questions: How do you respond to a non-compliant device?
How do you balance security and usability?
codeSnippet: notify -> grace period -> restrict/remediate -> confirm return to compliant

title: Asset reconciliation approach
summary: Explain matching logic, duplicate handling, source priority, and data trust improvement.
questions: How do you improve reconciliation quality?
What fields make strong identifiers?
codeSnippet: match on serial/device id -> normalize names -> merge duplicates cautiously

title: GRC control mapping approach
summary: Discuss how controls map to citations, risks, assessments, and follow-up actions.
questions: How do you explain control mapping?

title: Workspace quick-action thinking
summary: Explain how analysts use context and quick actions to shorten time to resolution.
questions: How does Workspace support shift-left?

codeSnippet: review 360 context -> trigger quick action -> document outcome

title: Velocity flow redesign
summary: Describe how to redesign frontline work with fewer prompts and better scan-first execution.
questions: How do you simplify a warehouse workflow?
